title: penstemon triphyllus
intro: Penstemon triphyllus, also known as the narrow-leaved beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae), native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Penstemon triphyllus has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Penstemon triphyllus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
